LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEROME RIPPERDA AND JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 5, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO PROVIDE THE DEFENDANTS AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPT. 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM JUNE 27 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN OVERRULING THE DEMURRER. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-05-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MTN TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTEROGGS FILED BY DEFENDANT RIPPERDA, JEROME JOSEPH JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA HEARING SET FOR DEC-19-2012 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS JEROME RIPPERDA AND JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTEROGGATORIES - THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS BEFORE IT THE DECLARATION BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN WHICH IT IS STATED THAT PLAINTIFF ADRIANA GUTIERREZ HAS NOW SERVED VERIFIED RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS' FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE. HOWEVER, THE SERVICE OF RESPONSES DOES NOT DIVEST THE COURT OF JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. (CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1348(A).) MS. GUTIERREZ'S ABSENCE AND HER FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH BASIC DUTIES OF DISCOVERY CAUSED DEFENDANTS TO INCUR FEES AND COSTS THAT WOULD HAVE BEEN AVOIDED ENTIRELY HAD SHE COMPLIED WITH HER DISCOVERY DUTIES IN A TIMELY MANNER. ACCORDINGLY, SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF ADRIANA GUTIERREZ ONLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $740.00 (REPRESENTING FOUR HOURS OF ATTORNEY TIME TOTALING $680.00 IN ATTORNEY FEES, AND $60 FOR THE MOTION FILING FEE), TO BE PAID TO DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: JIM WEIXEL, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER SECRET GRANDSON'S PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM REQUIREMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 7, 2012 BECAUSE THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS. PLAINTIFFS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM MAY 31, 2012 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS' NOTICE OF HEARING AND PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM REQUIREMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 20, 2012. THE COURT HAS EXERCISED ITS DISCRETION TO CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH THE REPLY PAPERS. THEREFORE, DEFENDANT IS GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY, IF IT WISHES, TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS ON OR BEFORE JUNE 15 WITH COURTESY COPIES DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302 THE SAME DAY. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT PERMITTED TO FILE ANY FURTHER PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM REQUIREMENT FILED BY RESPONDENT RIPPERDA, JEROME JOSEPH JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER SECRET GRANDSON'S NOTICE OF HEARING AND PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM REQUIREMENT IS GRANTED. PETITIONERS HAVE DEMONSTRATED MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE AND/OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT ENTITLING THEM TO RELIEF FROM THE GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIMS REQUIREMENTS. RESPONDENT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED PREJUDICE UPON WHICH THE COURT SHOULD DENY RELIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Mtn To Compel Response To Form Interoggs
Off Calendar Dec-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL B. ALWEISS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1276 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - DEFENDANT JEROME RIPPERDA, JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA Mtn To Compel Response To Form Interoggatories - The motion to compel is denied. The Court has before it the declaration by Plaintiff's counsel in which it is stated that Plaintiff Adriana Gutierrez has now served verified responses to Defendants' Form Interrogatories, Set One. However, the service of responses does not divest the Court of jurisdiction over Defendants' request for monetary sanctions. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a).) Ms. Gutierrez's absence and her failure to comply with basic duties of discovery caused Defendants to incur fees and costs that would have been avoided entirely had she complied with her discovery duties in a timely manner. Accordingly, sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff Adriana Gutierrez only, in the amount of $740.00 (representing four hours of attorney time totaling $680.00 in attorney fees, and $60 for the motion filing fee), to be paid to Defendants' counsel within 30 days of the date of this order. =302/jpt
